settings.title: Spring Equinox Morning Workshop
settings.excerpt: Mark the turn of the season with a two hour Saturday morning practice followed by tea together.
settings.body:
The equinox is a natural moment to pause and reset, and we are holding a special workshop to mark it. Join us on a Saturday morning for two unhurried hours on the mat.

Twice a year the day and night fall into balance, and the spring one in particular feels like a turning point, the moment the light starts to win. It is a fitting time to shake off the inward, heavy feeling that winter tends to leave.

How the morning unfolds

The session moves from gentle waking movement into a flowing sequence and ends with a long, deep rest. It is designed to shake off winter heaviness and meet the lighter months with energy.

The shape of the two hours:

  • A slow, gentle start to wake the body.
  • A flowing sequence to build a little warmth and energy.
  • A long, supported rest to close.

Tea afterwards

Afterwards we will share tea and something to eat in the studio. It is as much about community as it is about the practice, and some of the nicest conversations happen over that cup of tea.

Spaces are limited by the size of our room, so please reserve early. All levels are welcome and props are provided, so come along if you practise often or have not unrolled a mat in months.
